title: RMHP
point: RMHP stands for Raw Material Handling Plant.

title: OHP
point: OHP stands for Ore Handling Plant.

title: OBBP
point: OBBP stands for Ore Bedding and Blending Plant.

title: Steel Plant Role
point: RMHP is the starting point of an integrated steel plant.

title: Coal Exception
point: RMHP handles all major raw materials except coal.

title: Major Raw Material
point: Iron ore is a major raw material in integrated steel plants.

point: Limestone is a major raw material in integrated steel plants.

point: Dolomite is a major raw material in integrated steel plants.

title: Major Raw Material
point: Manganese ore is a major raw material in integrated steel plants.

point: Ferro manganese is used as a raw material.

title: Major Raw Material
point: Silico manganese is used as a raw material.

title: Major Raw Material
point: Quartzite is used as a raw material.

title: Major Raw Material
point: Coal is a major steel plant raw material.

point: RMHP performs crushing operations.

point: RMHP performs bedding operations.

point: RMHP performs blending operations.

title: Blending Objective

title: Supply Objective

title: Continuity Objective

title: Buffer Stock

title: Wagon Unloading

title: Preparation
point: RMHP prepares raw materials through crushing and screening.

title: Mixed Breeze Coke
point: Mixed breeze coke is generated inside the plant.

title: Mill Scale
point: Mill scale is generated inside the plant.

title: Flue Dust
point: Flue dust is generated inside the plant.

title: LD Slag
point: LD slag is generated inside the plant.

title: Pellet Usage
point: Low-grade ores and fines are utilized through pelletization.

title: Fines Generation
point: Mechanized mining generates large quantities of fines.

title: Fines Limitation
point: Fines cannot be directly charged into blast furnaces.

title: Beneficiation
point: Low-grade ores are beneficiated after crushing and grinding.

title: Blue Dust
point: Blue dust deposits remain largely unused due to fineness.

title: Agglomeration Method
point: Sintering is an agglomeration method.

title: Agglomeration Method
point: Pelletization is an agglomeration method.

title: Agglomeration Method
point: Briquetting is an agglomeration method.

title: Agglomeration Method
point: Nodulizing is an agglomeration method.

title: Agglomeration
point: Agglomeration forms larger bodies from smaller particles.

title: Agglomeration Goal
point: Agglomeration improves utilization of mineral fines.

title: Energy Saving
point: Agglomeration improves reduction efficiency.

title: Coke Saving
point: Agglomeration reduces coke rate.

title: Environment Benefit
point: Agglomeration utilizes waste in-plant fines.

title: Pelletization
point: Pelletization is an agglomeration process by balling.

title: Pellet Size
point: Typical pellet size is 8–20 mm.

title: Additive
point: Bentonite is used during pelletization.

title: Additive
point: Lime may be used during pelletization.

title: Firing Temperature
point: Green pellets are fired at 1200–1350°C.

title: Pellet Type
point: Pellets may be acidic.

title: Pellet Type
point: Pellets may be basic.

title: Pelletization Need
point: Pelletization is the only practical route for fine concentrates.

title: Pellet Advantage
point: Pellets have better tumbling index than parent ore.

title: Pellet Advantage
point: Pellets substitute lump ore in blast furnaces.

title: Future Technology
point: Pelletization is expected to dominate Indian steel industry.

title: Reducibility
point: Pellets possess good reducibility.

title: Porosity
point: Pellet porosity is typically 25–30%.

title: Reducibility Benefit
point: High porosity causes faster reduction.

title: Permeability
point: Pellets possess good bed permeability.

title: Drawback
point: Low angle of repose is a pellet drawback.

title: Metallization
point: High porosity increases metallization.

title: Heat Requirement
point: Pellets require 35–40% less heat than sintering.

title: Chemical Uniformity
point: Pellets have uniform chemical composition.

title: LOI
point: Pellets have very low loss on ignition.

title: Handling Benefit
point: Pellets are easy to handle and transport.

title: Strength
point: Pellets possess high strength.

title: Transport Benefit
point: Pellets can be transported over long distances.

title: Disintegration
point: Pellets resist disintegration.

title: Pellet Fe
point: Minimum Fe in BF pellet is 65%.

title: SiO2+Al2O3
point: Maximum SiO2+Al2O3 in pellet is 5%.

title: Al2O3 Limit
point: Maximum Al2O3 in pellet is 0.60%.

title: Na2O Limit
point: Maximum Na2O in pellet is 0.05%.

title: K2O Limit
point: Maximum K2O in pellet is 0.05%.

title: TiO2 Limit
point: Maximum TiO2 in pellet is 0.10%.

title: Mn Limit
point: Maximum Mn in pellet is 0.10%.

title: P Limit
point: Maximum phosphorus in pellet is 0.04%.

title: S Limit
point: Maximum sulfur in pellet is 0.02%.

title: V Limit
point: Maximum vanadium in pellet is 0.05%.

title: Basicity Ratio
point: Basicity=(CaO+MgO)/(SiO2+Al2O3).

title: Basicity Value
point: Pellet basicity is 0.40.

title: Moisture Limit
point: Fair season pellet moisture maximum is 4%.

title: Moisture Limit
point: Monsoon pellet moisture maximum is 6%.

title: Screen Analysis
point: +16 mm pellet fraction maximum is 5%.

title: Screen Analysis
point: -16 mm +9 mm pellet fraction minimum is 85%.

title: Screen Analysis
point: -9 mm +6.35 mm pellet fraction maximum is 7%.

title: Screen Analysis
point: -5 mm pellet fraction maximum is 5%.

title: Tumble Index
point: Minimum tumble index is 94%.

title: Abrasion Index
point: Maximum abrasion index is 5%.

title: Swelling
point: Maximum swelling is 20%.

title: Compression Strength
point: Minimum compression strength is 250 kg per pellet.

title: Porosity Requirement
point: Minimum pellet porosity is 25%.

title: Reducibility Requirement
point: Minimum pellet reducibility is 60%.

title: Raw Material Quality
point: Right quality raw material ensures maximum output.

title: Cost Reduction
point: Good raw material quality lowers operating cost.

title: Sampling
point: Incremental samples are collected from consignments.

title: Sampling Unit
point: Samples are collected using auto samplers.

title: Sample Preparation
point: Quartering and coning are used for sample preparation.

title: Iron Ore Lump SiO2

title: Iron Ore Lump Al2O3

point: -10 mm fraction maximum is 5%.

title: Iron Ore Lump Size
point: +40 mm fraction maximum is 5%.

title: Iron Ore Fines SiO2

point: Iron ore fines Al2O3 is 2.8–3.3%.

title: Iron Ore Fines Size
point: +10 mm fraction maximum is 5%.

title: Iron Ore Fines Size
point: -1 mm fraction maximum is 30%.

point: BF dolomite contains 5% SiO2.

point: SMS limestone contains maximum 1.5% SiO2.

point: SMS dolomite contains 23.5% MgO.

point: SMS dolomite contains 2.5% SiO2.

title: Mn Ore Quality
point: Manganese ore contains minimum 30% Mn.

title: Coke Breeze Quality
point: Fixed carbon in coke breeze exceeds 70%.

title: Coke Breeze SiO2
point: Coke breeze contains 12–15% SiO2.

title: Coke Breeze Moisture
point: Coke breeze moisture is 10–15% maximum.

title: Coke Breeze Size
point: Coke breeze size is less than 15 mm.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Raw materials arrive from mines.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Materials are unloaded by wagon tippler or track hopper.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Materials undergo sampling.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Materials are stacked in designated beds.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Materials pass through screening units.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Materials undergo bedding and blending.

title: RMHP Flow
point: Processed material is dispatched to customers.

title: Wagon Tippler
point: Wagon tippler mechanizes wagon unloading.

title: Car Pusher
point: Car pusher positions wagons in tipplers.

title: Track Hopper
point: Track hopper enables manual wagon unloading.

title: Stacker
point: Stacker forms storage beds.

title: SCR
point: Stacker-cum-reclaimer performs stacking and reclaiming.

title: Bucket Wheel
point: Bucket wheel reclaims materials from beds.

title: Transfer Car
point: Transfer car shifts equipment between beds.

title: Screens
point: Screens separate desired material sizes.

title: Crushers
point: Crushers reduce material size.

title: Belt Conveyor
point: Belt conveyors transport materials.

title: Logistics Planning
point: Raw material planning is essential for smooth operation.

title: Advance Planning
point: Raw material requirements must be communicated early.

title: Agency
point: Traffic and Raw Material Department participates in planning.

title: Agency
point: RMD participates in planning.

title: Agency
point: PPC participates in planning.

title: Agency
point: Finance participates in planning.

title: Agency
point: Materials Management participates in planning.

title: Agency
point: Railways participate in planning.

title: Railway Role
point: Indian Railways is the major transport link.

title: Wagon Type

title: Wagon Type
point: BOXC wagons are unloaded by wagon tippler.

title: Wagon Type
point: BOST wagons are unloaded by wagon tippler.

title: Wagon Type
point: NBOY wagons are unloaded by wagon tippler.

title: Wagon Type
point: BOBS wagons are unloaded by track hopper.

title: Wagon Type
point: NBOBS wagons are unloaded by track hopper.

title: Bed Formation
point: Bed formation occurs through stacker to-and-fro movement.

title: Layer Control
point: Stacker speed controls number of layers.

title: Homogeneity
point: More layers improve bed homogeneity.

title: Standard Deviation
point: More layers reduce standard deviation.

title: Blending
point: Blending homogenizes raw materials mechanically.

title: Homogenization
point: Homogenization rises rapidly beyond 400 layers.

title: Layer Effect
point: Homogenization becomes nearly constant after 580 layers.

title: Screening Need
point: Iron ore lump screening protects blast furnace performance.

title: Undersize Effect
point: -10 mm fraction adversely affects blast furnace operation.

title: Incoming Lump
point: Incoming lump ore may contain 15–20% fines.

title: Screen Mesh
point: Vibratory screen mesh size is 10×10 mm.

title: Sized Ore
point: Screened iron ore lump is called sized ore.

title: Base Mix
point: Base mix is a near homogeneous mixture.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Iron ore fines are used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Crushed limestone is used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Crushed dolomite is used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Crushed coke is used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: LD slag fines are used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Mill scale is used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Flue dust is used in base mix.

title: Base Mix Material
point: Return sinter is used in base mix.

title: Proportioning Bin
point: Individual storage bunkers are called proportioning bins.

title: Base Mix Ratio
point: Iron ore fines constitute 70–80% of base mix.

title: Iron Ore Fines Fe
point: Iron ore fines Fe content is around 62–64%.

title: Flux
point: Flux is a mixture of limestone and dolomite.

title: Flux Size
point: Crushed flux contains over 90% minus 3 mm fraction.

title: Flux Function
point: Flux neutralizes gangue in blast furnace.

title: Flux Function
point: Flux improves slag formation reactions.

title: Flux Function
point: Flux acts as a binder in sinter making.

title: Flux Share
point: Flux constitutes 12–16% of base mix.

title: Crusher Type
point: Hammer crushers crush limestone and dolomite.

title: Coke Size Requirement
point: Coke for base mix requires 85% minus 3 mm fraction.

title: Mixed Breeze Coke
point: Coke is received from coke ovens and blast furnace.

title: Nut Coke
point: +12.5 mm coke fraction becomes nut coke.

title: Crusher Type
point: Two-stage roll crushers process coke breeze.

title: Coke Share
point: Coke share in base mix is 5–6%.

title: BOF Slag Share
point: BOF slag share in base mix is 3.5–4%.

title: Waste Share
point: Metallurgical waste share in base mix is about 1%.

title: Metallurgical Waste
point: Mill scale is a metallurgical waste.

title: Metallurgical Waste
point: Flue dust is a metallurgical waste.

title: Metallurgical Waste
point: Sludge is a metallurgical waste.

title: Metallurgical Waste
point: Spillage is a metallurgical waste.

point: Sinter Plant is a RMHP customer.

title: Customer
point: Calcining/Refractory Plant is a RMHP customer.

title: RMHP Benefit
point: RMHP provides consistent raw material quality.

title: Centralization
point: RMHP provides centralized raw material facilities.

title: Fast Unloading
point: RMHP enables mechanized faster unloading.

title: Sizing Benefit
point: RMHP provides sizing facilities.

title: Screening Benefit
point: Screening minimizes undersize in lump ore.

title: Blending Benefit
point: Bedding and blending improve consistency.

title: Quality Tracking
point: Input quality can be tracked over time.

title: Base Mix Benefit
point: RMHP prepares base mix for sinter plants.

title: Waste Utilization
point: RMHP promotes metallurgical waste utilization.

title: Dust Hazard
point: RMHP is highly dust prone.

title: PPE
point: Dust masks are mandatory in RMHP.

title: PPE
point: Safety goggles are essential in RMHP.

title: PPE
point: Safety helmets are essential in RMHP.

title: PPE
point: Safety shoes are essential in RMHP.

title: Dust Control
point: Dust extraction systems are installed.

title: Dust Control
point: Dust suppression systems are installed.

title: DFDS
point: Dry Fog Dust Suppression systems are used in some plants.

title: Housekeeping
point: Housekeeping is a major operational challenge.

title: Reuse
point: Spillage material is collected and reused.

title: Scrap Disposal
point: Scrap conveyor belts require proper disposal.

title: Safety Benefit
point: Good housekeeping improves equipment safety.

title: Safety Benefit
point: Good housekeeping improves personal safety.

title: RMHP Core
point: Unload–Stack–Screen–Blend–Dispatch.

title: Flow Sequence
point: Mines→Unload→Sample→Bed→Screen→Blend→Customer.
